title: Bridge Essay: Human brain effects of DMT assessed via EEG-fMRI.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.

**Human brain effects of DMT assessed via EEG-fMRI.**. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A.. PMID: 36940333. DOI: 10.1073/pnas.2218949120. Psychedelics have attracted medical interest, but their effects on human brain function are incompletely understood. In acomprehensive, within-subjects, placebo-controlled design, we acquired multimodal neuroimaging [i.e., EEG-fMRI (electroencephalography-functional MRI)] data to assess the effects of intravenous (IV) N,N-Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) on brain function in 20 healthy volunteers.
